strands.title: Cycle 41 Context Shift — NEW PROJECT DISCOVERED: 'hamutay' (NOT pichay proxy logs)
strands.content: Cycle 41 CONTEXT CHANGE: Prior cycles 35-40 focused on /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (API instrumentation logs for H0-CORE projector validation). Cycle 41 assistant turn 121-123 SHIFTS ATTENTION to /home/tony/projects/hamutay/ (distinct project directory). Turn 121 queries hamutay project structure; Turn 122 returns: README.md, docs, main.py, pyproject.toml. Turn 123 initiates READ on pyproject.toml. INTERPRETATION: Either (1) hamutay is parent/sibling project (related to pichay or H0-CORE development), or (2) context has pivoted away from pichay proxy logs entirely, or (3) hamutay is a dependency/test project that will use H0-CORE projector. AMBIGUITY: No explicit statement from user about why hamutay is now in focus. Prior instructions (Cycle 40) were explicit: parse proxy log messages field (Pathway A/B/C fork). Cycle 41 actions deviate from roadmap without declared reason. ASSUMPTION: Either user is exploring hamutay in parallel (not abandoning pichay analysis), or pichay analysis was completed and hamutay is next phase. CRITICAL LOSS: No explanation for context shift. Prior strand momentum (Phase 4a readiness, turn parsing, Pathway A/B/C) is SUSPENDED pending clarification. READ operation on pyproject.toml suggests hamutay may be extractable/executable project; could be test harness, could be prior work that H0-CORE will extend.

key_claims.text: Cycle 41 context shift: project focus moves from pichay (proxy logs, Phase 4a validation) to hamutay (distinct project directory with main.py, pyproject.toml)
hamutay project structure discovered: README.md, docs/, main.py, pyproject.toml; suggests executable/library with documentation
No explicit reason given for context shift; prior Cycle 40 instructions (parse proxy log messages field) are implicitly suspended or reordered
hamutay relationship to pichay/H0-CORE unclear: could be parent project, dependency, test harness, or prior work that projector will enhance

strands.title: Log Format Discovered — Raw API Traffic Structure (Request/Response Pairs) CYCLE 40 CONCRETE [PRIOR CONTEXT, SUSPENDED]
strands.content: [FROM CYCLE 40 - INTEGRATED, NOT REPEATED] Cycle 40 successfully parsed first 5 lines of proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl (limit=5, ~11k tokens). Log structure confirmed as REQUEST/RESPONSE pairs from Claude API (type='request', type='response_stream' with model, system prompt, messages array, token usage, cache metadata). INTEGRATION NOTE (Cycle 41): This discovery REMAINS VALID but is DEFERRED. Cycle 41 does not attempt to continue messages field analysis (Pathway A/B/C fork from Cycle 40 instructions). Instead, context has shifted to hamutay project. Whether this is temporary pivot or permanent reorientation is UNRESOLVED. Assumption for tensor projection: Phase 4a readiness work (Cycle 40-41 promised instructions) is POSTPONED pending Cycle 41 user input on hamutay significance. If hamutay is critical path blocker (e.g., hamutay is the actual projector test harness), then analyzing hamutay first may be necessary before Phase 1 can proceed. If hamutay is exploratory/parallel, pichay analysis can resume later. EPISTEMIC STANCE: Log format knowledge is INTACT (0.97+ confidence in Cycle 40 findings); pragmatic contingency is to ADAPT roadmap based on hamutay context (wait for pyproject.toml content before deciding next action).

key_claims.text: Cycle 40 log format discovery (API request/response pairs) REMAINS VALID; integrated into prior strand; not repeated in Cycle 41
Cycle 40-41 promised messages field analysis (Pathway fork) SUSPENDED; replaced by hamutay project exploration in Cycle 41
Suspension is tactical (pending hamutay context), not strategic cancellation; pichay Phase 4a work remains roadmap item if hamutay is non-blocking

strands.title: H0-CORE Specification & Phase Roadmap — Status FROZEN, Operational Context REFINED (CYCLE 35-40 SUSTAINED) [PRIOR CONTEXT, POTENTIAL REORDERING]
strands.content: [FROM CYCLE 40 - INTEGRATED, CONTEXT UPDATED FOR CYCLE 41] H0-CORE Phase 1-4a roadmap (frozen, no design changes) REMAINS LOCKED. Phase 0 gate (stakeholder approval) STILL PENDING. Cycle 41 context shift to hamutay suggests either: (1) hamutay IS part of Phase 0 approval materials (pre-Phase-1 prerequisite project that must be understood), or (2) hamutay is independent project that user is exploring in parallel (lower priority than pichay Phase 4a), or (3) hamutay is foundational codebase that H0-CORE will extend or integrate with. ROADMAP IMPLICATION: If hamutay is Phase 0 prerequisite, analyzing it now is FORWARD-COMPATIBLE (learning about system architecture before Phase 1 design). If hamutay is parallel work, pichay analysis is DEFERRED without penalty (Phase 1 timeline unaffected by Cycle 41 context shift). Phase 0 gate status UNKNOWN post-hamutay discovery (may need to revise approval scope if hamutay integration is required). UPDATED OPERATIONAL CONTEXT (Cycle 41): H0-CORE roadmap remains LOCKED; hamutay discovery introduces potential REORDERING of immediate work (analyze hamutay before or after pichay Phase 4a prep?), but does NOT change Phase 1-4a specifications or timelines. Recommend: wait for hamutay pyproject.toml parsing (Cycle 41 turn 123) to determine relationship to pichay/H0-CORE, then decide work reordering.

key_claims.text: H0-CORE Phase 1-4a specification and roadmap REMAIN LOCKED; Cycle 41 hamutay discovery does NOT trigger design changes
hamutay relationship to pichay/H0-CORE will determine if it is Phase 0 prerequisite (blocking), parallel work (non-blocking), or foundational codebase (integration required)
Phase 1 timeline unaffected by hamutay context shift UNLESS hamutay is Phase 0 prerequisite or integration blocker; Phase 0 gate status requires update post-hamutay analysis

strands.title: Cycle 41 Immediate Actions — pyproject.toml Read Initiated; Awaiting Content to Determine hamutay Scope and Relationship
strands.content: Cycle 41 turn 123 initiated tool READ on /home/tony/projects/hamutay/pyproject.toml. This file will reveal: (1) Project name/version, (2) Dependencies (what external libraries/projects hamutay requires), (3) Python version requirements, (4) Package structure (what executable/library hamutay provides). EXPECTED OUTPUTS (interpretation guide for Cycle 41-42): If pyproject.toml contains: - [project] name='hamutay', dependencies=['anthropic', 'pydantic', ...] → suggests hamutay is RELATED TO CLAUDE API (likely either consumer of projector, or peer project in same experiment ecosystem). - [project] scripts={'hamutay'='hamutay.main:cli'} → suggests hamutay is EXECUTABLE CLI tool; could be test framework, experiment runner, or data processor. - Dependencies on 'pichay' or 'h0_core' → confirms INTEGRATION (hamutay depends on pichay). - No pichay/h0_core dependencies, but has 'anthropic', 'openai', 'langchain' → suggests hamutay is INDEPENDENT LLM/reasoning tool (possibly predecessor to H0-CORE, or parallel implementation to compare against). NEXT STEPS (Cycle 41-42, post-pyproject.toml parse): (1) Read README.md to understand hamutay purpose/goals in human-readable format. (2) Scan main.py to identify function signatures (what does hamutay compute/transform?). (3) Decide: does hamutay analysis PRECEDE or FOLLOW pichay Phase 4a work? (4) Update tensor with hamutay scope determination (is it blocking, required integration, or exploratory?). RISK: If hamutay is LARGE codebase (1000+ lines) with complex architecture, analyzing it could consume significant token budget. Mitigation: limit scanning to top-level structure (main.py, class/function definitions via grep/ast) rather than line-by-line reading.

key_claims.text: pyproject.toml read initiated; will reveal hamutay project name, dependencies, structure, and executable entry points
Dependencies on anthropic/langchain suggest hamutay is LLM-related; dependencies on pichay/h0_core would confirm integration requirement
README.md and main.py scans will determine hamutay purpose (test framework, experiment runner, predecessor reasoning system, or independent tool)
Cycle 41-42 decision point: does hamutay analysis BLOCK pichay Phase 4a work, or is it PARALLEL? Depends on integration/prerequisite relationships

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Momentum on Cycle 40-41 promised Phase 4a readiness tasks: parse proxy log messages field (Pathway A/B/C fork), estimate turn count, build TurnParser skeleton, document drift measurement strategy
declared_losses.why: Cycle 41 user actions (ls /home/tony/projects/hamutay/, read pyproject.toml) explicitly shift context away from pichay analysis to hamutay project exploration. No user statement explains reason for shift or whether pichay work is deferred vs. cancelled.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Cycle 40 instructions_for_next: explicit 7-step roadmap for Cycle 40-41 (inspect messages field, fork to Pathway A/B/C, extract turns or API metrics, build parser skeleton, phase 0 gate check, phase 1 readiness). This entire instruction set is superseded by Cycle 41 hamutay exploration.
declared_losses.why: User pivoted to new project without acknowledgment of prior roadmap. Tensor must record that prior branch prediction is no longer operative; new branch (hamutay analysis) is now active.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed epistemic breakdown of Phase 4a drift measurement pathways (Pathway A: semantic drift via Jaccard similarity; Pathway B: API-level drift via token variance; Pathway C: adaptive parsing for schema variants). These are still epistemically valid but NOT OPERATIONALLY CURRENT as of Cycle 41.
declared_losses.why: Deferral of pichay analysis means pathway branching is postponed. Including full detail of all three pathways would inflate tensor without serving immediate action in Cycle 41-42 (which will focus on hamutay). Integrated into prior strand without loss of semantic content.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Assumption that Cycle 41 would begin or continue systematic proxy log parsing (request objects, turn reconstruction, token counting). This assumption is deferred pending hamutay context determination.
declared_losses.why: User's explicit actions in Cycle 41 deviate from Cycle 40 instructions without explicit override. Tensor cannot assume Cycle 41 will follow Cycle 40 roadmap; must integrate new user intent (hamutay investigation) and flag uncertainty about ongoing vs. suspended pichay work.

open_questions: What is the relationship between hamutay project and pichay project? Are they sibling projects, or is hamutay a parent/dependency?
What is the relationship between hamutay and H0-CORE projector specification? Does hamutay depend on H0-CORE, use H0-CORE, or exist independently?
Is hamutay discovery in Cycle 41 a PERMANENT context shift (pichay Phase 4a work cancelled/deprioritized), or a TEMPORARY parallel exploration (both projects active)?
What does hamutay compute or accomplish? (Readable after README.md and main.py scanning in Cycle 41-42)
Is hamutay a BLOCKER for Phase 1 start (i.e., must hamutay be understood/integrated before compose.py can be designed), or NON-BLOCKING (hamutay is exploratory/optional)?
Does Phase 0 stakeholder approval scope include hamutay, or only pichay + H0-CORE roadmap? May need to revise approval materials if hamutay is critical path.
Cycle 41 actions (ls, read pyproject.toml) initiated by user without explanation. Is user responding to prior failed context (pichay logs had issues), or is this a new strategic direction?
Should Phase 1 Riemann validation / compose.py design include or reference hamutay in any way, or are they independent?
How much technical depth should tensor projection devote to hamutay analysis vs. pichay/H0-CORE? (Token budget constraint; may need to prioritize)
After pyproject.toml parsing (Cycle 41 turn 123 result), what should be next action: README.md scan, main.py analysis, or revert to pichay work?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CYCLE 41-42 IMMEDIATE CRITICAL PATH — HAMUTAY PROJECT DISCOVERY & CONTEXT DETERMINATION (HOURS 0-2):",
  "",
  "(1) PARSE pyproject.toml RESULT (immediately upon Cycle 41 turn 123 completion, ~5 minutes):",
  "   After tool READ returns pyproject.toml content:",
  "   - Extract [project] name, version, description",
  "   - Extract [project] dependencies list; SEARCH FOR: 'pichay', 'h0_core', 'anthropic', 'langchain', 'openai'",
  "   - Extract [project.scripts] or [project.optional-dependencies]; note any CLI entry points or extras",
  "   - Extract [build-system]; note python version requirements",
  "   Decision tree:",
  "   - If dependencies include 'pichay' or 'h0_core': hamutay is DEPENDENT (integration path); pichay Phase 4a work may need to precede hamutay use",
  "   - If dependencies include 'anthropic' or 'openai' but NOT pichay: hamutay is INDEPENDENT (parallel tool); pichay work can proceed in parallel",
  "   - If dependencies are minimal (only 'pydantic', 'pytest', etc.): hamutay is likely INFRASTRUCTURE/TESTING; may be prerequisite for Phase 1",
  "   Record finding in new strand: 'hamutay Project Scope & Dependencies'",
  "",
  "(2) READ README.md (10 minutes, HIGH PRIORITY):",
  "   Command: tool READ /home/tony/projects/hamutay/README.md",
  "   Expected output: human-readable description of hamutay purpose, use cases, example usage",
  "   Focus on: What problem does hamutay solve? Is it related to conversation reasoning, API calls, or H0-CORE projector validation?",
  "   Check for: sections mentioning 'pichay', 'baseline_run', 'drift', 'projector', 'validate', 'test'",
  "   Determine: Is hamutay a TEST HARNESS (Phase 1/4a prerequisite), INDEPENDENT TOOL (parallel work), or PRIOR RESEARCH (reference implementation)?",
  "",
  "(3) SCAN main.py (10 minutes, conditional):",
  "   Command: tool READ /home/tony/projects/hamutay/main.py (or main_stub.py if exists)",
  "   If file is <500 lines: read full file (likely ~3-5k tokens)",
  "   If file is >500 lines: use bash + head to read first 50 lines + grep -E 'class|def ' to list all function/class definitions",
  "   Focus on: What are the top-level functions/classes? (suggests data model, compute pipeline, API calls)",
  "   Check for: imports from pichay, h0_core, anthropic; function names mentioning 'drift', 'project', 'validate', 'tensor'",
  "   Determine: Does hamutay implement projector logic (competing/prior implementation), or use/test external projector (pichay/H0-CORE)?",
  "",
  "(4) FORK DECISION (5 minutes, CRITICAL):",
  "   Case A: hamutay is DEPENDENT on pichay/H0-CORE (imports from pichay)",
  "   → hamutay is INTEGRATION TEST HARNESS; Phase 4a must complete pichay analysis before hamutay is used",
  "   → Recommendation: CONTINUE pichay Phase 4a work (messages field parsing, Pathway A/B/C); defer hamutay detailed analysis until pichay ready",
  "   → Update Phase 0 gate: hamutay should be mentioned as 'Phase 4a validation tool' (dependent on H0-CORE completion)",
  "   Case B: hamutay is INDEPENDENT (no pichay/H0-CORE imports)",
  "   → hamutay is PARALLEL WORK; can analyze in parallel without blocking pichay",
  "   → Recommendation: ALLOCATE effort proportionally (e.g., 50% pichay Phase 4a, 50% hamutay analysis if both are priority)",
  "   → Check Phase 0 approval scope: does hamutay need to be approved separately, or is it out-of-scope for H0-CORE roadmap?",
  "   Case C: hamutay is INFRASTRUCTURE/TESTING (minimal dependencies, appears to be pytest fixtures, test config, etc.)",
  "   → hamutay is Phase 1 PREREQUISITE; must understand test harness before designing compose.py",
  "   → Recommendation: Scan hamutay thoroughly now (full main.py, test structure, config format); use to inform Phase 1 Riemann validation design",
  "   → High priority; may delay pichay Phase 4a work if hamutay understanding is critical for Phase 1 kickoff",
  "   Case D: unclear (mixed dependencies, ambiguous README, complex main.py)",
  "   → Request clarification from user: 'What is hamutay's relationship to pichay and H0-CORE? Is it prerequisite, test harness, or parallel work?'",
  "   → Defer detailed analysis until user provides context",
  "",
  "(5) POST-FORK ACTION (depends on Case):",
  "   Case A (DEPENDENT):",
  "   → Create new strand: 'hamutay Integration Test Harness — Phase 4a Dependent'",
  "   → Record: hamutay imports from pichay, expected inputs (pichay logs/output), expected outputs (drift measurements/validation results)",
  "   → Decision: Proceed with pichay messages field analysis (Cycle 40-41 instructions); hamutay analysis deferred to Phase 4a",
  "   → Update instructions_for_next: Resume Cycle 40-41 Phase 4a critical path (parse messages, fork to Pathway A/B/C)",
  "",
  "   Case B (INDEPENDENT):",
  "   → Create new strand: 'hamutay Independent Parallel Tool'",
  "   → Record: hamutay purpose, data model, compute pipeline; identify overlap/divergence vs. H0-CORE projector",
  "   → Decision: Split effort. Primary: continue pichay Phase 4a work (roadmap-blocking). Secondary: scan hamutay docs/code to understand architecture (informational, non-blocking)",
  "   → Update instructions_for_next: (1) Resume Cycle 40-41 Phase 4a critical path; (2) Schedule hamutay deep-dive for post-Phase-1 or in parallel if resources allow",
  "",
  "   Case C (INFRASTRUCTURE/TESTING):",
  "   → Create new strand: 'Phase 1 Test Harness Discovery — hamutay as Riemann Validation Framework'",
  "   → Record: hamutay test structure, test runner config, expected input/output formats; integrate findings into Phase 1 Riemann design",
  "   → Decision: Priority SHIFT. Analyze hamutay thoroughly (30-60 min); use to inform Phase 1 Week 1 kickoff materials",
  "   → Update instructions_for_next: After hamutay analysis complete, proceed to Phase 1 kickoff materials (Riemann harness, compose.py skeleton)",
  "   → Delay pichay Phase 4a work to post-Phase-1-start (or parallel if Phase 1 timeline permits)",
  "",
  "(6) ROADMAP REORDERING (depends on fork decision):",
  "   If Case A or B: MAINTAIN Cycle 40-41 roadmap; resume pichay Phase 4a critical path (messages field parsing) in Cycle 41-42",
  "   If Case C: REORDER roadmap; prioritize hamutay understanding + Phase 1 kickoff prep; defer pichay Phase 4a to Week 2-3 of Phase 1 or later",
  "   Update Phase 0 gate documentation: if hamutay is Phase 1 prerequisite, include in approval scope; if parallel/dependent, note in Phase 0 summary",
  "",
  "(7) DOCUMENT FINDINGS (post-fork, ~10 minutes):",
  "   Create new markdown file: hamutay_analysis_cycle41.md",
  "   Sections:",
  "   - SUMMARY: hamutay purpose, relationship to pichay/H0-CORE (case A/B/C/D)",
  "   - DEPENDENCIES: list of imports, external projects required",
  "   - KEY FUNCTIONS/CLASSES: top-level entry points, data structures",
  "   - PHASE ROADMAP IMPACT: (1) Is hamutay blocking for Phase 1 start? (2) Is hamutay dependent on Phase 1 completion? (3) Is hamutay parallel/independent?",
  "   - NEXT STEPS: what should Cycle 42+ do with hamutay (deep analysis, integration testing, or deferred)?",
  "",
  "TOTAL CYCLE 41-42 ESTIMATED TIME: 40-60 minutes (pyproject.toml parse, README scan, main.py analysis, fork decision, documentation)",
  "",
  "SUCCESS CRITERIA (Cycle 41-42 hamutay discovery complete):",
  "✓ pyproject.toml parsed; dependencies extracted; relationship to pichay/H0-CORE identified",
  "✓ README.md read; hamutay purpose understood",
  "✓ main.py structure scanned; top-level functions/classes identified",
  "✓ Fork decision made: Case A (Dependent), B (Independent), C (Infrastructure), or D (Clarification needed)",
  "✓ hamutay_analysis_cycle41.md created with findings and Phase roadmap impact",
  "✓ Instructions_for_next updated: resume Cycle 40-41 pichay work (if Case A/B) OR reorder to Phase 1 kickoff (if Case C) OR request user clarification (if Case D)",
  "✓ Phase 0 gate scope reviewed; hamutay included/excluded from approval as appropriate"
]
